title: Rich Raw Algerian King Prawns Tagine
description: A fatty and fluffy Algerian dish featuring raw king prawns, this tagine is perfect for a sick day, with ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients to help soothe and nourish the body. It is a broiled dish that combines the flavors of Africa and the Mediterranean.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, butter, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the raw king prawns to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and arrange the prawns in a single layer.
Broil the prawns for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they are opaque and slightly charred.
Remove the prawns from the oven and squeeze with lemon juice.
Serve the prawns hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Traditionally, this dish is served with a side of crusty bread or over couscous.
To make it more substantial, add some roasted vegetables like carrots and zucchini to the baking sheet with the prawns.
